purpose: Single source of truth for Project Dokkaebi enterprise-readiness evaluation, issue generation, worktree execution, pull request review, merge, and re-evaluation.

designStandards.name: Carbon Design System color guidance
requiredInterpretation: Use docs/design/carbon-ui-baseline.md as the repository-owned interpretation for UI PRs.
Use role-based color tokens rather than hard-coded product colors for UI surfaces.
Use Carbon light and dark theme layering rules when Dokkaebi introduces product UI.
Use the core blue action family intentionally, with support colors reserved for status and alerts.
Require accessible contrast for text, icons, graphical elements, focus states, and data visualization.

scoringScale.0: Required for company use but absent or not evidenced.
scoringScale.1-30: Direction exists, but the implementation or operating evidence is too thin for company use.
scoringScale.31-60: Partially implemented, mostly manual, sandbox-oriented, or not yet auditable enough.
scoringScale.61-80: Usable for internal pilots, with important enterprise controls still missing.
scoringScale.81-95: Strong enough to become an internal standard after targeted proof and automation.
scoringScale.96-100: Production-ready, auditable, supportable, and validated against realistic operating scenarios.

workflow.sourceOfTruth: This file is the readiness criteria source of truth. GitHub Project Status remains the lifecycle state source of truth for work items.
workflow.phaseModel: For each area below, open or update a GitHub issue from nextIssues as a backlog candidate. The issue becomes dispatchable only after it is added to an approved GitHub Project with lifecycle/admission fields. Then create a short-lived worktree and branch, implement with evidence, open a pull request, merge only after required validation, and update currentPercent and nextIssues from the new evidence.
workflow.issueContract: Each issue must cite the relevant area id, scope, currentPercent, targetPercent, acceptance criteria, validation requirements, authority requirements, metadata hygiene expectation, and result evidence expected from the worker.
workflow.worktreeContract: Each issue is solved in an isolated worktree or worker workspace. Branch names follow docs/policies/git-governance.md and do not use actor prefixes.
workflow.pullRequestContract: Each pull request must include context, decision, why, validation, residual risks, git status, and issue linkage.
workflow.mergeContract: Merge only after the applicable repository checks pass and approval requirements in docs/policies/git-governance.md are satisfied.
workflow.evaluationLoop: After merge, re-run the relevant validation, reassess all criteria from evidence, create follow-up issues for any area or critical capability below 100%, and repeat until every area and critical capability reaches 100%.
workflow.selfImprovementContract: The readiness loop may change its own criteria, validation, issue contract, or phase model when evidence shows the loop is failing to drive work, omits required enterprise evidence, creates ambiguous issues, cannot safely score a criterion, or repeatedly blocks on the same operational gap.
improvementTriggers: A readiness issue is not dispatchable because required scope, authority, validation, result evidence, or metadata hygiene is missing.
A criterion cannot be scored from repository, GitHub, CI, runtime, or operations evidence.
A validator passes while a documented loop requirement is absent or unenforced.
A review, incident, or failed PR identifies a repeatable gap in the loop itself.
The loop reaches an enterprise standard that is undefined and requires a Human decision before scoring can continue.
improvementEvidence: Open or update a GitHub issue that names the loop gap and its acceptance criteria.
Capture RED evidence showing the missing or weak loop behavior before changing criteria or validation.
Make the smallest criteria, documentation, issue-template, or validator change that closes the gap.
Run readiness, contract, package, git-governance, and relevant targeted validation before PR.
Open and merge a PR before using the improved loop to rescore readiness.

metadataHygiene.publicBranchRule: Use <type>/<scope-slug> branch names without actor prefixes.
metadataHygiene.publicCommitRule: Commit messages must explain process, decision, validation, and residual risk without exposing internal automation labels.
metadataHygiene.publicIssueRule: Issue and pull request metadata must describe product work, not private tool execution details.

areas.name: Architecture and contracts
areas.enterpriseStandard: Manager, Fire, Hammer, GitHub Project state, credential, approval, and result-packet boundaries are complete, versioned, validated, and exercised by at least one live or replayed operating scenario.
evaluationQuestions: Can a new Manager implementation pass the contract without reading private project memory?
Can a worker result packet be rejected deterministically when required evidence is missing?
Do contract validators catch drift across architecture, workflow, policy, and templates?
evidenceRequired: Example accepted and rejected worker result packets

areas.name: Core orchestration
areas.enterpriseStandard: Fire reliably discovers, admits, dispatches, monitors, retries, and closes work without duplicate execution, stale state, or silent loss across restarts.
gaps: Sandbox issue processing transcript is not yet captured as auditable closeout evidence.
evaluationQuestions: Can Fire restart without duplicate dispatch or lost retry intent?
Can worker route selection explain why each target was selected or skipped?
Can failed workers produce actionable state and evidence for Human Review?
evidenceRequired: Unit and integration tests for admission, route selection, retry, and closeout

nextIssues.title: Add sandbox issue processing transcript gate
acceptanceCriteria: A sandbox transcript shows one issue moving through discovery, admission, dispatch readiness, worker result evidence, Manager review, and closeout.
The transcript records approval-gate status and authority boundaries for every live or sandbox operation.
The transcript is referenced by readiness criteria and can be replayed or audited without private memory.
validationRequired: Targeted sandbox transcript validation named by the implementation
nextIssues.authorityRequirement: docs-and-approved-sandbox-validation only after the sandbox target and allowed operations are explicitly approved. Live worker, Docker, Kubernetes, remote host, credential, production, deployment, and GitHub Project control-plane mutation require explicit Human approval under docs/policies/authority-and-safety.md.

areas.name: Infrastructure and platform
areas.enterpriseStandard: Development, sandbox, staging, and production topologies are reproducible, isolated, monitored, backed up, and recoverable with least-privilege worker routes.
gaps: Topology, HA, backup, restore, and DR evidence shape plus a local replay with measured RPO/RTO results are captured, but approved credential-free sandbox restore evidence is still pending.
Docker and Kubernetes route maturity is uneven.
Remote worker bootstrap is approved only for named development and sandbox targets.
evaluationQuestions: Can a new environment be rebuilt from documented commands and checked state?
Are local, SSH, Docker, and Kubernetes workers registered with clear capabilities and cleanup rules?
Can Fire recover its state and workspaces after host or process failure?

nextIssues.title: Run approved credential-free sandbox restore drill
acceptanceCriteria: Run an approved credential-free sandbox restore drill using the documented topology and local replay evidence shape.
Capture sandbox target, restore point, restore steps, measured RPO and RTO, DR roles, validation output, approval-gate status, cleanup receipt, residual risk, and next action.
Update docs/enterprise-readiness/criteria.json only from captured sandbox evidence.
validationRequired: Targeted sandbox drill validation named by the implementation
nextIssues.authorityRequirement: approved-sandbox-validation only after Human approval records the exact sandbox target, operations, credentials-none statement, rollback path, and cleanup path. Remote host, Docker, Kubernetes, Proxmox, credential, production, deployment, infrastructure, and GitHub Project control-plane mutation require explicit Human approval under docs/policies/authority-and-safety.md.

areas.name: Development and quality
areas.enterpriseStandard: Every change has focused tests, deterministic validation, risk-based coverage, release evidence, and regression protection for adjacent contracts.
gaps: Important runtime modules are not fully covered by default quality gates.
Live end-to-end, soak, and failure injection tests are not routine merge gates.
Release candidate and rollback validation are incomplete.
evaluationQuestions: Does every readiness criterion map to an executable or reviewable validation?
Are high-risk runtime paths covered by tests that fail for the right reason?
Can a release candidate be verified and rolled back from documented evidence?
evidenceRequired: Targeted test output for changed areas

nextIssues.title: Create risk-based quality gate matrix for runtime modules
acceptanceCriteria: Matrix lists orchestration, credential, GitHub adapter, worker provider, and UI risk surfaces.
Each risk surface has required tests or explicit accepted risk.
Validation command verifies the matrix exists and names the required surfaces.
validationRequired: Targeted validation named by the issue implementation
nextIssues.authorityRequirement: No additional authority by default for docs-only or local validation work. If implementation touches credentials, infrastructure, workers, merge, deploy, or production operations, explicit Human approval is required under docs/policies/authority-and-safety.md.

areas.name: Security and authority
areas.enterpriseStandard: All credentials, approvals, worker capabilities, high-risk operations, audit events, and emergency paths are least-privilege, revocable, observable, and fail-closed.
gaps: Secret lifecycle, rotation, revocation drills, and access reviews are not fully operationalized.
Threat modeling and prompt-injection controls need a documented enterprise baseline.
Multi-tenant RBAC design exists, but runtime enforcement and access-review drill evidence are not captured.
evaluationQuestions: Can each credential grant be traced to an approval boundary and revoked?
Can a worker perform only the operations authorized by its ticket and route?
Are high-impact operations blocked without explicit Human approval evidence?

nextIssues.title: Write enterprise credential lifecycle and revocation drill
acceptanceCriteria: Document covers token classes, owners, storage, rotation, revocation, and audit evidence.
Development and sandbox auth exception remains narrow and explicitly approved.
A dry-run revocation checklist can be executed without exposing secrets.
validationRequired: Targeted validation named by the issue implementation
nextIssues.authorityRequirement: No additional authority by default for docs-only or local validation work. If implementation touches credentials, infrastructure, workers, merge, deploy, or production operations, explicit Human approval is required under docs/policies/authority-and-safety.md.

areas.name: Management and governance
areas.enterpriseStandard: GitHub Project fields, issue intake, Human approval, PR review, result packets, and closeout reconciliation are governed by reproducible policy and auditable roles.
gaps: GitHub branch protection and project rules are documented but not fully codified as reproducible settings.
Role matrix is documented in the RBAC model, but stale Human Review escalation needs stronger operating policy.
Closeout reconciliation between issue, PR, result packet, and project state is not automated.
evaluationQuestions: Can a non-automation team member understand why an item is in its current state?
Can role boundaries explain who may authorize, dispatch, merge, and audit work?
Can mismatches between issue status, PR state, and result evidence be detected?

nextIssues.title: Add GitHub Project governance matrix and closeout reconciliation plan
acceptanceCriteria: Role matrix names project owner, approver, Fire operator, Hammer operator, security reviewer, and auditor.
Plan defines stale Human Review and mismatch handling.
Validation checks that required roles and reconciliation surfaces are present.
validationRequired: Targeted validation named by the issue implementation
nextIssues.authorityRequirement: No additional authority by default for docs-only or local validation work. If implementation touches credentials, infrastructure, workers, merge, deploy, or production operations, explicit Human approval is required under docs/policies/authority-and-safety.md.

areas.name: Logging and observability
areas.enterpriseStandard: State, logs, metrics, traces, alerts, dashboards, and audit exports let operators detect, diagnose, prove, and improve system behavior without reading private worker memory.
gaps: Central metrics backend design exists, but measured ingestion, storage, query, dashboard, alert, and tracing evidence are not captured.

Operational dashboards and alert rules are designed and mapped to on-call routing, but they are not evaluated against replayed or sandbox metrics and are not tied to an approved production paging backend.
Audit export design exists, but metrics evidence retention and export verification remain unproven.
evaluationQuestions: Can an operator identify stalled dispatch, unhealthy workers, and credential denial within minutes?
Can metrics and logs be correlated by issue, route, worker, session, and commit?
Can audit records be exported without leaking secrets?

nextIssues.title: Define observability metrics catalog and alert rules
acceptanceCriteria: Metrics catalog covers dispatch latency, queue depth, worker health, retries, failures, credential denials, and review age.
Alert rules map to operator actions and severity.
Docs define redaction and retention expectations.
validationRequired: Targeted validation named by the issue implementation
nextIssues.authorityRequirement: No additional authority by default for docs-only or local validation work. If implementation touches credentials, infrastructure, workers, merge, deploy, or production operations, explicit Human approval is required under docs/policies/authority-and-safety.md.

areas.name: Operations and SRE
areas.enterpriseStandard: The service has SLOs, incident response, on-call paths, runbooks, rollout and rollback procedures, capacity planning, and routine drills.
gaps: Dedicated SLO/SLA boundary, incident response, on-call paging and alerting boundaries, central metrics design, release, rollback, capacity, soak, and drill evidence shapes are documented, but paging implementation and routine approved drills are not operational.
Capacity, soak, and central metrics thresholds exist, but long-running measured soak or metrics tests are not yet captured from a local or approved-sandbox run.
Release and rollback procedures are documented, but approved drill evidence is still required before production service quality can be claimed.
evaluationQuestions: What happens when Fire is down, GitHub API is rate-limited, or all workers are unhealthy?
Who is paged and what runbook do they follow?
What are acceptable dispatch latency and recovery objectives?

nextIssues.title: Run approved release and rollback drill
acceptanceCriteria: Drill identifies release candidate, staged rollout step, rollback trigger, operator, communication surface, and closeout evidence.
Drill proves rollback decision and recovery path through captured command output, transcript, or approved sandbox evidence.
Readiness criteria are updated only from evidence produced by the drill.
validationRequired: Targeted release/rollback drill validation named by the implementation
nextIssues.authorityRequirement: No additional authority by default for docs-only or local validation work. If implementation touches credentials, infrastructure, workers, merge, deploy, or production operations, explicit Human approval is required under docs/policies/authority-and-safety.md.

areas.name: Compliance and audit
areas.enterpriseStandard: The system can produce control-mapped, retained, tamper-evident evidence for approvals, credentials, code changes, worker execution, and operational incidents.
gaps: Control mapping, package shape, one audit review walkthrough, immutable audit export design, and one local replay verification drill exist, but signed export verification, retention enforcement, and immutable object storage are not operational.

Retention, redaction, ownership, source-link, and manifest-hash metadata are replayed from sanitized repository evidence, but signing-key management, rotation, revocation, and automated export generation remain incomplete.
Formal SOC 2, ISO 27001, or external-control mapping remains unapproved and out of scope.
evaluationQuestions: Can an auditor reconstruct who approved a worker action and why?
Can evidence be exported with redaction and integrity proof?
Do policies map to common enterprise controls?

nextIssues.title: Add signed immutable audit export storage and key management
acceptanceCriteria: Define signed manifest storage, signing-key ownership, rotation, revocation, and verification cadence.
Capture evidence for one approved signed export verification or a clearly bounded sandbox substitute with no secret leakage.
Record retention enforcement, redaction review, owner review, cleanup, residual risk, and next action.
Update docs/enterprise-readiness/criteria.json only from captured evidence.
validationRequired: Targeted signed immutable audit export validation named by the implementation
nextIssues.authorityRequirement: docs-only design by default. Any credential, production, infrastructure, worker, remote host, Proxmox, Docker, Kubernetes, deployment, or GitHub Project control-plane mutation requires explicit Human approval under docs/policies/authority-and-safety.md.

areas.name: Productization and UX
areas.enterpriseStandard: Admins, approvers, operators, auditors, and worker authors can install, configure, monitor, approve, troubleshoot, and learn the product without relying on undocumented operator context.
gaps: Admin UI and guided setup are incomplete.
Role-based onboarding and troubleshooting are documented and validated, but not yet implemented as guided UI flows.
Operator journeys for triage, approval, result review, and troubleshooting are still document-led rather than embedded in product UI.
evaluationQuestions: Can a new admin install and configure a sandbox without maintainer help?
Can a Human reviewer approve or reject work from GitHub issue and PR surfaces confidently?
Can an operator troubleshoot worker failures from documented symptoms?
evidenceRequired: Admin UI or CLI screenshots when available

nextIssues.title: Implement guided onboarding UI and admin wizard
acceptanceCriteria: Implement or identify a first-party guided onboarding UI or admin wizard for project setup.
Capture role-specific screenshots or visual QA evidence for admin, approver, operator, auditor, and worker-author flows.
Connect UI or CLI onboarding to GitHub Project setup checks, repository setup checks, approval review actions, result-packet closeout actions, and troubleshooting next actions.
Update docs/enterprise-readiness/criteria.json after implemented onboarding evidence exists.
validationRequired: Targeted UI, CLI, or visual QA validation named by the implementation
nextIssues.authorityRequirement: No additional authority by default for docs-only or local validation work. If implementation touches credentials, infrastructure, workers, merge, deploy, or production operations, explicit Human approval is required under docs/policies/authority-and-safety.md.

areas.name: Design system and visual quality
areas.enterpriseStandard: All Dokkaebi UI surfaces use a documented Carbon Design System baseline for color, interaction states, accessibility, and enterprise information density.
gaps: Only the Symphony observability dashboard currently proves Carbon token usage, responsive mobile layout, contrast evidence, and CJK-safe dynamic text on a first-party UI surface.
Reusable component-library token proof is not complete across all Dokkaebi UI surfaces.
CI visual regression capture and full cross-browser visual regression matrix are not complete.
evaluationQuestions: Do UI surfaces use Carbon tokens and theme layering instead of arbitrary palettes?
Do focus, hover, selected, active, disabled, error, warning, and success states meet accessibility requirements?
Does the interface feel like a dense operational product rather than a marketing page?
evidenceRequired: Desktop and mobile visual QA captures

nextIssues.title: Add component library and CI visual regression gate
acceptanceCriteria: Define a reusable Carbon component or token inventory for first-party UI surfaces instead of relying on one dashboard screen only.
Add CI visual regression evidence or artifact capture for desktop and mobile dashboard views.
Include contrast and state coverage for focus, hover, selected, active, disabled, error, warning, success, status, and data elements where applicable.
Document residual cross-browser matrix gaps or add the first cross-browser validation lane.
Update docs/enterprise-readiness/criteria.json after component-library and CI visual regression evidence exists.
validationRequired: Targeted component inventory and CI visual regression validation named by the implementation
nextIssues.authorityRequirement: docs-only, local UI validation, or CI artifact validation unless a later PR explicitly records higher approved authority. Deployment, production write, credential, infrastructure, worker, remote host, Docker, Kubernetes, and GitHub Project control-plane mutation require explicit Human approval under docs/policies/authority-and-safety.md.
resultEvidence: Component inventory or design token library evidence
CI visual regression output or artifact links
Desktop and mobile visual QA evidence

criticalCapabilities.name: Incident Response
acceptanceCriteria: Run an incident response tabletop or approved sandbox drill using the SRE and on-call paging baselines.
Capture severity, commander, communication, mitigation, postmortem, alert routing decision, and follow-up evidence.
Update docs/enterprise-readiness/criteria.json after drill evidence exists.
validationRequired: Targeted validation named by the issue implementation
criticalCapabilities.authorityRequirement: No additional authority by default for docs-only or local validation work. If implementation touches credentials, infrastructure, workers, merge, deploy, or production operations, explicit Human approval is required under docs/policies/authority-and-safety.md.

criticalCapabilities.name: On-call, paging, and alerting
criticalCapabilities.nextIssueTitle: Run on-call paging backend and alert routing drill
acceptanceCriteria: Record the approved paging backend, escalation roster, escalation windows, and quiet-hours behavior before any live route is exercised.
Route representative dispatch-latency, recovery-time, stale-review, worker-capacity, validation-failure, approval-boundary, and compliance-evidence alerts to the selected sinks.
Capture alert evaluation input, routing decision, quiet-hours decision, delivery or dry-run output, cleanup, residual risk, and next action.
Update docs/enterprise-readiness/criteria.json only from captured drill evidence.
validationRequired: Targeted alert routing drill validation named by the implementation
criticalCapabilities.authorityRequirement: docs-only or local dry-run validation by default. Live alerting service, infrastructure, credential, worker, remote host, Docker, Kubernetes, deployment, production, and GitHub Project control-plane mutation require explicit Human approval under docs/policies/authority-and-safety.md.

criticalCapabilities.name: SLO and SLA
criticalCapabilities.nextIssueTitle: Run central metrics backend sandbox verification
acceptanceCriteria: Produce or replay measured dispatch-latency, recovery-time, review-age, and availability posture evidence using docs/operations/service-level-objectives.md and docs/operations/central-metrics-backend.md.
Capture metric query output, fallback evidence, error-budget result, alert linkage, approval-gate status, cleanup, residual risk, and next action.
Update docs/enterprise-readiness/criteria.json after measured SLO evidence exists.
validationRequired: Targeted central metrics sandbox or local replay validation named by the implementation
criticalCapabilities.authorityRequirement: No additional authority by default for docs-only or local validation work. If implementation touches credentials, infrastructure, workers, merge, deploy, or production operations, explicit Human approval is required under docs/policies/authority-and-safety.md.

Update docs/enterprise-readiness/criteria.json only from captured sandbox evidence.

Remote host, Docker, Kubernetes, Proxmox, credential, production, deployment, infrastructure, and GitHub Project control-plane mutation require explicit Human approval under docs/policies/authority-and-safety.md.

Record retention enforcement, redaction review, owner review, cleanup, residual risk, and next action.

criticalCapabilities.authorityRequirement: docs-only design by default. Any credential, production, infrastructure, worker, remote host, Proxmox, Docker, Kubernetes, deployment, or GitHub Project control-plane mutation requires explicit Human approval under docs/policies/authority-and-safety.md.

acceptanceCriteria: Produce or replay representative dispatch, recovery, review-age, worker-capacity, approval, validation, and audit-export metrics using docs/operations/central-metrics-backend.md.

Capture ingestion output, storage/query output, dashboard or parsed view evidence, alert-rule evaluation evidence, retention/cardinality checks, approval-gate status, cleanup, residual risk, and next action.
